seo.title: Building Code Generation with Haystack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build code generation with Haystack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building code generation pipelines with Haystack enables LLM agents to produce, execute, and refine code iteratively, powering advanced developer tools. However, high-risk scenarios like sandbox execution and credential handling demand robust guardrails to prevent syntax errors, security breaches, and hallucinations. Vex integration ensures reliable outputs through comprehensive verification at every pipeline stage.

architectureOverview.heading: Code Generation Architecture with Haystack
architectureOverview.description:
The typical Haystack code generation pipeline starts with a user prompt routed to a retriever that fetches relevant code examples or documentation from a vector store. This context feeds a code generator LLM (e.g., CodeLlama) producing executable code, which passes through a sandbox executor for safe runtime evaluation. Results from execution—including stdout, stderr, and test outcomes—trigger an iterative refinement loop via a critic agent that diagnoses failures and prompts regeneration until validation passes.

Key components include a PromptNode for orchestration, CustomCodeExecutor for sandboxing, and VexGuardrailNode for verification. The pipeline handles iterative flows using conditional routing based on execution success, test coverage, and safety checks. This modular design supports scaling to complex multi-file codebases with dependency management.
architectureOverview.diagram:
User Prompt → Retriever (FAISS) → Code Generator (CodeLlama)
                     ↓
              Vex Guardrails (Safety/Syntax)
                     ↓
             Sandbox Executor (Docker/Pyodide)
                     ↓
              Test Validator (Pytest/Unittest)
                     ↓
             Critic Agent → [Iterate | Success]

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: LLM generates code with syscalls, file writes, or network requests bypassing sandbox
risks.mitigation: Vex code safety scanner + isolated Docker execution

risks.name: Syntax errors halting execution
risks.description: Invalid Python syntax crashes sandbox, blocking iteration

risks.name: Credential leakage in generated code
risks.description: Hardcoded API keys or secrets from training data contamination

risks.name: Unsafe dependency installation
risks.description: pip install of malicious packages during execution

implementation.title: Basic Code Generation Pipeline
implementation.description: Core pipeline with retriever, generator, and executor components
implementation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from haystack.components.builders import PromptBuilder
from haystack.components.generators import HuggingFaceLocalGenerator
from haystack.components.retrievers.in_memory import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever

code_pipeline = Pipeline()
code_pipeline.add_component("retriever", InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever())
code_pipeline.add_component("prompt_builder", PromptBuilder(template="Write Python code for: {{query}} using context: {{documents}}"))
code_pipeline.add_component("generator", HuggingFaceLocalGenerator(model="codellama/CodeLlama-7b-hf"))
code_pipeline.connect("retriever", "prompt_builder.documents")
code_pipeline.connect("prompt_builder", "generator")

implementation.title: Sandbox Code Executor Component
implementation.description: Custom component safely executes generated code with timeout and resource limits

implementation.title: Test Validation Component
implementation.description: Automatically generates and runs pytest validations against generated code

implementation.title: Iterative Refinement Orchestrator
implementation.description: Conditional routing for code refinement based on execution and test results
implementation.code:
from haystack.components.routers import PromptTemplateRouter
from haystack import Pipeline

refine_pipeline = Pipeline()
refine_pipeline.add_component("executor", SandboxExecutor())
refine_pipeline.add_component("validator", TestValidator())
refine_pipeline.add_component("router", PromptTemplateRouter([
    {"condition": "{{validator.passed}}", "template": "Success!"},
    {"condition": "else", "template": "Fix this code: {{code}}. Errors: {{failures}}"}
]))
refine_pipeline.connect("executor.execution_result", "validator.code")
refine_pipeline.connect("validator", "router")

vexIntegration.title: Vex Guardrail Custom Component
vexIntegration.description: Add Vex as pipeline component for comprehensive code verification

vexIntegration.title: Pipeline with Vex Integration
vexIntegration.description: Insert Vex guardrails between generator and executor
vexIntegration.code:
from haystack import Pipeline

full_pipeline = Pipeline()
full_pipeline.add_component("generator", HuggingFaceLocalGenerator())
full_pipeline.add_component("vex_guardrail", VexGuardrailNode())
full_pipeline.add_component("executor", SandboxExecutor())

full_pipeline.connect("generator", "vex_guardrail.code")
full_pipeline.connect("vex_guardrail", "executor")

# Only execute safe code
full_pipeline.add_conditional_connections(
    "vex_guardrail.safe",
    ["executor", "end"],
    ["safe", "unsafe"]
)
